label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out if following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. So the steps are: take 7 steps, then 8 steps, then 10 steps. Then turn around twice, which would make me face the original direction again, because each turn around is 180 degrees, so two turns would be 360 degrees, back to the original facing. Then take 5 steps. Then turn around once again. So let's break it down step by step. First, I start at point A, facing a certain direction, say north. Let's assume each step is in a straight line in the direction I'm facing. 1. Take 7 steps north. So I'm 7 units north of A. 2. Take 8 steps north. Now I'm 7+8=15 units north. 3. Take 10 steps north. Now 15+10=25 units north. 4. Turn around. Now facing south. 5. Turn around again. Back to facing north. 6. Take 5 steps north. Now 25+5=30 units north. 7. Turn around. Now facing south. So after all these steps, I'm 30 units north of the starting point and facing south. To return to the starting point, I would need to take 30 steps south, but the instructions don't say that.
